title: ageratina gentryana
intro: Ageratina gentryana, also known as Mexican mint or Gentry's ageratina, is a perennial plant native to Mexico, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting digestive health. Ageratina gentryana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties.
